title: Paraguay Unveil 2026 FIFA World Cup Squad
original_text:
Paraguay have revealed their 26-man squad for the 2026 World Cup in North America. This will be their first appearance at the tournament since 2010 in South Africa, when they defeated Japan on penalties in the Round of 16 before narrowly losing 1-0 to eventual champions Spain in the quarter-finals.

Paraguay head into the World Cup in Group D alongside the United States, Australia and Türkiye knowing that they will not be seen as favourites, but their approach is unmistakable. The manager of the team, Gustavo Alfaro, is doubling down on work ethic, structure and experience whilst trusting a tight defensive unit and a bright spark of attacking players to make the difference when chances come their way.

At 31, Gustavo Gómez remains at the heartbeat of Paraguay's defence and one of South America's most reliable centre-backs. As captain for both his club and country, he has built a reputation for leadership, composure and consistency. Known for his aerial dominance, strong duelling and calm playmaking, Gómez has excelled across Brazil, Italy and Argentina throughout his 15-year playing career. With 88 caps, his experience and organisation make him the foundation of "The White-Red's" defensive structure heading into the World Cup.

Omar Alderete is a player who brings a different edge to the Paraguayan backline, a left-footed defender who defends aggressively and reads the danger early. The 29-year-old is coming off a strong season in the Premier League with Sunderland as he helped them qualify for European Football for the first time since 1973, making 33 appearances and keeping 11 clean sheets in the process. Having the ability to step out, break up play and play forward under pressure makes Alderete a standout inclusion in Alfaro's defensive plans for the tournament.

Since returning to the MLS last year, Miguel Almirón has quickly re-established himself as the main creative outlet for Atlanta United. So far in the 2026 MLS season , the 32-year-old has produced 3 assists in 8 appearances whilst averaging 2.60 shots per game and constantly finding pockets of space to drive attacks forward. The former Newcastle United player currently has 75 Paraguayan caps to his name, and this combination of experience, pace and clever movementmakese Almirón one of the most vital attacking options for "La Albirroja" in North America.

Over the last four years, Julio Enciso has developed into one of Paraguay's most exciting and unpredictable young talents. The 22-year-old has enjoyed a strong 2025/26 campaign with Strasbourg in Ligue 1 , contributing 6 goals and 3 assists in 27 league appearances for the French club. His direct running, sharp footwork and willingness to shoot from distance make him a constant threat in the final third. The former Brighton & Ipswich player also made history by becoming the first player from Paraguay to be nominated for the Golden Boy Award and to be a finalist for the renowned FIFA Puskás Award for a spectacular goal he scored for Brighton against Manchester City . Enciso's blend of youthful energy and attacking flair gives the South American side a dynamic option to use at the tournament.

Now one of the more senior figures in Paraguay's attack, Antonio Sanabria has continued to offer a reliable presence during the 2025/26 season with Cremonese in Serie A . Despite the club being relegated and only getting one goal and one assist in the campaign, his goalscoring record in the CONMEBOL World Cup Qualifiers tells a different story, with four goals and one assist which made him the top Paraguayan goal-scorer in the qualifiers overall. The blend of experience, physical presence and clever positioning makes him a reliable option for Alfaro to choose from going into the summer.
